content.claim: Hungary's 2013 re-centralization of school governance (KLIK) transferred responsibility from local government to a central agency but did not decrease achievement inequalities between schools, suggesting that governance centralization alone is not a sufficient lever for rural/disadvantaged-school equity.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Finnish system reform lens
limitations: Outcome measurement not specified (test scores, graduation rates, subject-matter competency)
Post-2013 period length and comparison baseline (versus prior decentralized system) not precisely stated; confounding with FBO expansion and demographic decline occurring simultaneously
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
